violations.text: Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, chicken was placed covered and hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Chicken was uncovered and put in freezer to finish cooling at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025

The door to the outside, located in the back of the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 07/07/2025

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Bowls were removed from containers at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2025

The 2 door lowboy was observed holding an ambient temperature of 48 DF. Cold holding units must be maintained at a temperature of 41 DF or less. Establishment is to have unit serviced and to send inspector proof of repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the grill line have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2025
